title: Festuca kingiana
intro: Festuca kingiana, also known as King's fescue, is a type of grass that belongs to the family Poaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues and skin irritations. The bioactive constituents of Festuca kingiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. Traditional herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to soothe respiratory issues and promote relaxation.
taxonomy: Festuca kingiana is a species of grass in the family Poaceae. It belongs to the genus Festuca, which includes various species of grasses. The genus Festuca is further classified under the subfamily Pooideae. This classification is based on the plant's characteristics, such as its leaf and flower structure. Festuca kingiana is native to South America, and its classification reflects its unique and specific features among other Festuca species.
